title: Exquisite Tender Japanese Beef Shin Ramen
description: A delicate and light Japanese dish featuring tender beef shin, served in a rich and savory broth.

ingredients.name: Beef Shin (500g)
ingredients.notes: Look for a piece with a good balance of meat and bone.

ingredients.name: Kombu (dried kelp)
ingredients.notes: This will be used to make the dashi broth.

ingredients.name: Katsuobushi (dried bonito flakes)
ingredients.notes: Also used to make the dashi broth.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality, low-sodium soy sauce.

ingredients.name: Mirin (sweet Japanese cooking wine)
ingredients.notes: This will add a sweet and savory flavor to the broth.

ingredients.name: Sesame Oil
ingredients.notes: Used for cooking the beef shin.

ingredients.name: Bamboo Shoots
ingredients.notes: Used for added texture and flavor.

ingredients.name: Pork or Chicken Bones
ingredients.notes: Used to make the dashi broth.

ingredients.name: Sesame-Ginger Sauce (store-bought or homemade)
ingredients.notes: Used for added flavor on top of the noodles.

instructions: Season the beef shin with salt and pepper, then seal it in a sous-vide bag with 1 tablespoon of sesame oil.
Cook the beef shin in the sous-vide machine for 24 hours.
While the beef is cooking, make the dashi broth by simmering the kombu and pork or chicken bones in water for 30 minutes.
Strain the dashi broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Add the katsuobushi and mirin to the dashi broth and simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
Cook the ramen noodles according to the package instructions, then drain and set aside.
Thinly slice the beef shin against the grain.
Assemble the ramen by placing noodles in a bowl, then topping with sliced beef, bamboo shoots, and green onions.
Drizzle the sesame-ginger sauce over the top of the noodles.
Sprinkle sesame seeds over the top of the dish and serve immediately.
